name: AWS Systems Manager
description: This app integrates with AWS Simple Systems Manager (SSM) to remotely and securely manage the configuration of any EC2 instance or on-premise machine configured for SSM

actions.name: Execute Program
actions.description: Runs shell script command on a managed instance

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.description: The AWS Instance ID of where the command will run

parameters.name: platform_type
parameters.description: The operating system of the instance

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Single command to be executed on the instance

parameters.name: working_directory
parameters.description: The path to the working directory on your instance

parameters.name: timeout_seconds
parameters.description: If this time is reached and the command has not already started running, it will not run

parameters.name: comment
parameters.description: User-specified information about the command, such as a brief description of what the command should do

parameters.name: output_s3_bucket_name
parameters.description: The name of the existing S3 bucket where command execution responses should be stored

parameters.name: save_output_to_vault
parameters.description: Store the output of the command into the vault

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: Get File
actions.description: Retrieve a file from an AWS instance and save it to the vault

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.description: The AWS Instance ID of where to grab the file from

parameters.name: platform_type
parameters.description: The operating system of the instance

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Full path of the file to download (include filename)

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: Run Document
actions.description: Runs command using a public or custom SSM Document on one or more managed instances

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.description: The AWS Instance ID of where the command will run

parameters.name: document_name
parameters.description: The name of the Systems Manager document to run. This can be a public document or a custom document

parameters.name: document_hash
parameters.description: The Sha256 or Sha1 hash created by the system when the document was created

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: JSON object. The required and optional parameters specified in the document being run. Example: {"commands": ["ls"]}

parameters.name: working_directory
parameters.description: The path to the working directory on your instance

parameters.name: timeout_seconds
parameters.description: If this time is reached and the command has not already started running, it will not run

parameters.name: comment
parameters.description: User-specified information about the command, such as a brief description of what the command should do

parameters.name: output_s3_bucket_name
parameters.description: The name of the S3 bucket where command execution responses should be stored

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: List Commands
actions.description: Lists the commands ran by users of the AWS account

parameters.name: command_id
parameters.description: If provided, lists only the specified command

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.description: Lists commands issued against this instance ID

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return for this call

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: List Documents
actions.description: Lists one or more of the Systems Manager documents

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the document to search for (case sensitive)

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The owner of the document to search for

parameters.name: platform_type
parameters.description: The OS platform type that the document can execute on (i.e. Windows, Linux)

parameters.name: document_type
parameters.description: The type of document

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return for this call

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: Get Parameter
actions.description: Get information about a parameter by using the parameter name

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the parameter that you want to query

parameters.name: with_decryption
parameters.description: Check to decrypt values of secure string parameters. This flag is ignored for String and StringList parameter types

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: Add Parameter
actions.description: Adds a parameter to the AWS account's Parameter Store

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The fully qualified name of the parameter that you want to add to the system. Please refer to app documentation page for naming restrictions

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Information about the parameter that you want to add to the system. Optional but recommended

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The parameter value that you want to add to the system. Standard parameters have a value limit of 4 KB. Advanced parameters have a value limit of 8 KB

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of parameter that you want to add to the system. Items in StringList must be separated by a comma (,). SecureString parameters must use an AWS KMS Key ID

parameters.name: key_id
parameters.description: The KMS Key ID that you want to use to encrypt a SecureString parameter. Defaults to the AWS KMS key automatically assigned to your AWS account if no key ID is provided

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Overwrite an existing parameter

parameters.name: allowed_pattern
parameters.description: A regular expression used to validate the parameter value. For example, for String types with values restricted to numbers, you can specify the following: AllowedPattern=^d+$

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: Describe Instance
actions.description: Describes your AWS instance, including the instance's platform type

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.description: The AWS instance ID

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials
